2025-02-09T07:03:18.CONTENT_EN: Please broadcast the following message as soon as possible: The Transport Department announces at 07:00 am: To facilitate the “Hong Kong Marathon 2025” today, road closure and traffic diversions have been implemented at various locations on Hong Kong Island, in Kowloon and the New Territories, major road sections closed include: Hong Kong Island: roads in the vicinity of Central Ferry Piers, Wan Chai North, Causeway Bay and Victoria Park, both bound carriageways of the Central-Wan Chai Bypass Tunnel linking to and from the Island Eastern Corridor (IEC) and both bound carriageways of the IEC between Victoria Park Road and Shau Kei Wan, etc. Kowloon: the section of Nathan Road northbound between Salisbury Road and Argyle Street, Nathan Road southbound between Gascoigne Road and Salisbury Road, Argyle Street westbound between Nathan Road and Tong Mi Road, West Kowloon Highway southbound and the Hong Kong-bound tube of Western Harbour Crossing (WHC) etc. New Territories: the Kowloon-bound carriageways of Ting Kau Bridge, Cheung Tsing Highway, Cheung Tsing Tunnel, Tsing Kwai Highway, Nam Wan Tunnel and Stonecutters Bridge, etc. The Kowloon-bound tube of WHC remains open. The vehicles from Hong Kong Island/Kowloon/New Territories East heading to the Airport and Lantau Island are not affected by the above road closures. There are 209 daytime bus routes and 33 daytime green minibus (GMB) routes are currently being diverted, truncated, suspended or relocated on their stopping points. In addition, bus stops, taxi stands, public light bus/GMB stands within the closed roads and their vicinities have been gradually suspended. The traffic in the affected areas and the major roads in the vicinity will become significantly congested when comparing to normal Sundays. Motorists should avoid driving to the above affected areas. In case of traffic congestion, they should exercise patience, drive with care and follow the instructions of the Police on site. Members of the public who plan to go to the affected areas should plan their journey in advance, allow sufficient travelling time to avoid unexpected delay and use railway services as far as possible, and stay alert to the latest traffic news through the media. Members of the public are advised to pay attention to radio / TV announcements on the latest traffic news.
2025-02-09T07:03:18.CONTENT_CN: 請盡早播放以下消息: 運輸署於上午07時00分宣佈： 為配合今日舉行「香港馬拉松二零二五」，港島、九龍及新界多處現正實施封路及交通改道措施，主要封閉路段包括： 港島區：中環碼頭、灣仔北、銅鑼灣及維多利亞公園一帶的道路、連接東區走廊的中環及灣仔繞道隧道來回方向、以及介乎維園道及筲箕灣之間的東區走廊的來回方向等。 九龍區：介乎梳士巴利道與亞皆老街的彌敦道(北行)、介乎加士居道與梳士巴利道的彌敦道(南行)、介乎彌敦道與塘尾道之間的亞皆老街(西行)、西九龍公路(南行)及往港島方向的西區海底隧道管道等。 新界區：往九龍方向的汀九橋、長青公路、長青隧道、青葵公路、南灣隧道及昂船洲大橋等。 往九龍方向的西區海底隧道管道則維持開放，由港島／九龍／新界東前往機場及大嶼山方向的車輛不受上述封路影響。 現時，209 條日間巴士路線及33條日間專線小巴路線正實施改道、縮短服務、暫停服務或臨時遷站。此外，位於封路地區內及鄰近地區的巴士站、的士站、公共小巴/專線小巴站已逐步暫停使用。 在受影響地區及附近的主要道路的交通將會較平常的星期日顯著擠塞。駕駛人士請避免駕車前往受影響的地區。如遇交通擠塞時，駕車人士請保持忍讓及小心駕駛，並遵從在場警方的指示。市民如前往受影響地區，請提早計劃行程及預留額外的時間以免延誤，盡量使用鐵路服務，並留意傳媒廣播的最新交通消息。 市民請留意電台/電視廣播的最新交通消息。

2025-02-09T11:58:58.CONTENT_EN: Please broadcast the following message as soon as possible: The Transport Department announces at 011:45 am: Due to the “Hong Kong Marathon 2025”, apart from the Kowloon-bound carriageways of Ting Kau Bridge, Cheung Tsing Tunnel, Tsing Kwai Highway, Nam Wan Tunnel, Stonecutters Bridge, West Kowloon Highway (southbound) and Western Harbour Crossing (Hong Kong bound), various previously suspended road sections in Kowloon were re-opened in phases. In addition, Central-Wan Chai Bypass, Island Eastern Corridor, Victoria Park Road, Hing Fat Street, Electric Road, Lau Li Street and Tsing Fung Street on Hong Kong Island were re-opened in phases. Nevertheless, some road sections along the race routes remain suspended at present, which include the exit / entrance in Wan Chai on Central-Wan Chai Bypass (eastbound) and some roads in the vicinity of Central Ferry Piers, Wan Chai North and Causeway Bay areas. Currently, there are about 115 daytime bus routes and about 10 daytime green minibus (GMB) routes that observed the closed road sections are continued to be diverted, truncated, suspended or relocated on their stopping points. The traffic in the affected areas and adjacent roads may be more congested as compared with normal Sundays. Motorists are advised to avoid driving to the affected areas. Members of the public going to the aforesaid areas should plan their journeys early and allow sufficient time to avoid unexpected delay, and use railway service as far as possible. Members of the public are advised to pay attention to radio / TV announcements on the latest traffic news.
2025-02-09T11:58:58.CONTENT_CN: 請盡早播放以下消息: 運輸署於上午11時45分宣佈： 因應「香港馬拉松二○二五」賽事，除往九龍方向的汀九橋、長青隧道、青葵公路、南灣隧道、昂船洲大橋、西九龍公路(南行)及西區海底隧道(往香港方向)外，早前在九龍區封閉的多條道路已先後重開。 此外，港島區的中環及灣仔繞道、東區走廊、維園道、興發街、電氣道、琉璃街及清風街已先後重開，但現時沿賽道的部分路段仍然封閉，包括中環及灣仔繞道(東行)的灣仔出入口，以及中環碼頭、灣仔北及銅鑼灣一帶的部份道路。 現時，途經封閉路段的約115條日間巴士路線及約10條日間專線小巴路線繼續實施改道、縮短服務、暫停服務或臨時遷站安排。 受影響地區及附近道路的交通可能會較平常的周日擠塞，駕駛人士請避免駕車前往受影響的地區，市民請提早計劃行程及預留額外的行程時間以免延誤，並盡量使用鐵路服務。 市民請留意電台/電視廣播的最新交通消息。

2025-02-09T13:15:00.CONTENT_EN: Please broadcast the following message as soon as possible: The Transport Department announces at 1:15 pm: Due to the “Hong Kong Marathon 2025”, the Kowloon-bound carriageways of Ting Kau Bridge, Cheung Tsing Tunnel, Tsing Kwai Highway, Nam Wan Tunnel and Stonecutters Bridge; and West Kowloon Highway (southbound) and Western Harbour Crossing (Hong Kong bound), which were previously suspended in New Territories and Kowloon, were re-opened. At present, some road sections along the race routes remain suspended, which include the exit / entrance in Wan Chai of Central-Wan Chai Bypass (eastbound) and some roads in the vicinity of Central Ferry Piers, Wan Chai North and Causeway Bay areas. Currently, there are about 65 daytime bus routes and about 10 daytime green minibus (GMB) routes that observed the closed road sections are continued to be diverted, truncated or relocated on their stopping points. The traffic in the affected areas and adjacent roads may be more congested as compared with normal Sundays. Motorists are advised to avoid driving to the affected areas. Members of the public going to the aforesaid areas should plan their journeys early and allow sufficient time to avoid unexpected delay, and use railway service as far as possible. Members of the public are advised to pay attention to radio / TV announcements on the latest traffic news.

2025-02-09T14:00:00.CONTENT_EN: Please broadcast the following message as soon as possible: The Transport Department announces at 2:00 pm: All suspended roads on Hong Kong Island, in Kowloon and the New Territories in connection with the “Hong Kong Marathon 2025”, are now re-opened for traffic and all the affected public transport services have resumed normal.
